objective: Fix reusable governance-check diff-range handling under workflow_call so multi-commit PR governance checks cover the full change set, and propagate the explicit base SHA through governed repo callers.
scope_boundary: Add an explicit base SHA input to the reusable governance-check workflow and use it for all diff/log comparisons.
Update governed repo workflow callers to pass the correct base SHA for pull_request and push events.
Record the bug fix and close the governance issue once reusable workflow and caller propagation land.
out_of_scope: Reworking repo-specific conditional skips beyond passing the correct base SHA.
Replacing reusable workflow usage with copied local workflows.
research_summary: The reusable governance workflow runs via workflow_call, but its changed-file and commit-range logic currently depends on pull_request context or github.event.before. In workflow_call, github.event.before is often unset, so the workflow can fall back to HEAD~1 and inspect only the last commit of a multi-commit PR. The durable fix is to accept a caller-provided base SHA and use that single resolved value everywhere the reusable workflow diffs or reads commit ranges.

sprints.name: Sprint 1
sprints.goal: Make the reusable governance workflow base-sha aware.
tasks: Add a workflow_call input for base_sha.
Resolve a single diff base value inside the reusable workflow.
Use the resolved base value everywhere the workflow currently relies on pull_request context or github.event.before.
acceptance_criteria: Reusable governance-check accepts an explicit base SHA.
Changed-file, placeholder, FAIL_FAST, and PROGRESS row-count checks use the same resolved base SHA.
The workflow no longer silently falls back to HEAD~1 when the caller can provide a real base.

sprints.name: Sprint 2
sprints.goal: Propagate the fix to governed repo callers and close governance with evidence.
tasks: Update governed repo governance workflow callers to pass base_sha.
Record the failure pattern and closeout artifacts in governance.
Close the issue after reusable workflow and caller propagation are merged.
acceptance_criteria: All reusable workflow callers pass the correct base SHA.
Multi-commit PR governance checks can cover the full PR diff.
Governance records the fix and closes the issue with merged evidence.

specialist_reviews.focus: Validate that the right fix is caller-provided base SHA propagation, not another local fallback inside the called workflow.
specialist_reviews.summary: The reusable workflow cannot reliably infer PR base SHA under workflow_call. Passing base_sha from the caller is the correct fix, with a bounded fallback only for direct/non-PR invocation paths.

alternate_model_review.summary: This is a narrow reusable-workflow fix and did not require alternate-model review.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Fix reusable governance-check diff-base handling and propagate the base_sha input through governed repo callers before closing the issue.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Patch the reusable workflow, then update caller repos to pass base_sha and collect merged evidence.

material_deviation_rules: If any caller cannot pass base_sha cleanly, create a follow-up issue before closing.
If another diff-based check is found still bypassing the resolved base value, absorb it in this slice before closure.
